column1: === EXP-BENCH v3 (v4): Hub Pinning + Hardened Per-Query Cold ===
v3: 1537 pages, adj_index=781KB DRAM, BFS reorder
Fix: drain_prefetch() now resets orphaned LOADING entries (was causing deadlock)
label            ef    k  W   S   D   c%  recall   p50ms   p99ms     qps   phy/q   mis/q  hit/q   hit%    byp evfail
label            ef    k  W   S   D   c%  recall   p50ms   p99ms     qps   phy/q   mis/q  hit/q   hit%    byp evfail
--- Hub pinning (v3, per-query cold EXCEPT pinned pages) ---
NOTE: pool.clear() skips pinned entries. These configs are NOT fully per-query cold:
pinned hub pages persist across queries, providing partial cross-query state.
This isolates the value of pinning hub pages on top of intra-query page reuse.
label            ef    k  W   S   D   c%  recall   p50ms   p99ms     qps   phy/q   mis/q  hit/q   hit%    byp evfail  pinned
label            ef    k  W   S   D   c%  recall   p50ms   p99ms     qps   phy/q   mis/q  hit/q   hit%    byp evfail  pinned
--- Equal-budget comparison (v1 vs v3, same pool_bytes, per-query cold) ---
pool_bytes  layout   recall   p50ms   p99ms    qps    phy/q   mis/q  hit/q   hit%    byp evfail
Per-query cold (clear_per_query) now works correctly. Previous version deadlocked
because drain_prefetch() removed channel entries without resetting their LOADING
slots, causing has_loading() to spin forever.
Pinning 64-128 pages out of 1537 total (4-8%) gives 10-13% IO reduction.
The benefit is structural: hub pages (BFS-ordered, low page_ids) are frequently
accessed during graph traversal. Pinning them eliminates repeated loads.
pin128 requested 128 but only pinned 120 — 8 pages hit MAX_PINNED_PER_SET=4
In warm steady-state, cross-query caching already keeps hub pages hot.
Explicit pinning adds almost nothing — the cache naturally converges to
pinning the same pages via LRU/clock.
At 1MB per-query cold: v3 phy/q=135.4 vs v1 phy/q=209.4 (-35%)
These match v3 results from previous runs within noise.
All v3 configs show byp=0, evfail=0. The set-associative cache never saturates
with pinned entries because MAX_PINNED_PER_SET=4 leaves 4 ways for eviction.
v1-cold shows byp=51, evfail=65 — tiny 32KB pool can't hold working set.
p99=19.4ms is suspiciously high (vs 9.3-10.0 for other v3-warm configs).
p50=7.2ms is normal, suggesting a single-query tail spike. With nq=100,
p99 ≈ max, so one slow query dominates. Should rerun with nq≥500 to get
a stable p99 estimate before drawing conclusions about S4D16 tail behavior.
